## BSD Resources Repository
=====================================

### Description
This repository provides a comprehensive collection of resources for those interested in BSD (Berkeley Software Distribution) systems.

### Table of Contents
* [Cloud Providers](#cloud-providers)
* [Sites](#sites)
* [Operating Systems](#operating-systems)

## Cloud Providers
-----------------

BSD can be hosted on various cloud providers. The following providers have been verified to support BSD:

### AWS Lightsail

*   URL: <https://aws.amazon.com/lightsail>
*   Verified Date: 31 March 2026
*   Supported BSD Versions:
    *   FreeBSD 15.0
    *   FreeBSD 14.3
    *   FreeBSD 13.5

### Hawk Host

*   URL: <https://www.hawkhost.com>
*   Verified Date: 31 March 2026
*   Supported BSD Versions:
    *   FreeBSD 14
    *   FreeBSD 13

### Server Space

*   URL: <https://serverspace.io>
*   Verified Date: 31 March 2026
*   Supported BSD Versions:
    *   FreeBSD 14

### Vultr

*   URL: <https://www.vultr.com>
*   Verified Date: 31 March 2026
*   Supported BSD Versions:
    *   FreeBSD 15
    *   FreeBSD 14
    *   OpenBSD 7.8
    *   OpenBSD 7.7

## Operating Systems
--------------------

 BSD offers a variety of operating systems, each with its own strengths and weaknesses.

### DragonFly BSD

*   URL: <https://www.dragonflybsd.org>
*   Notes:
    *   A high-performance variant of FreeBSD.
    *   Known for its stability and security features.

### FreeBSD

*   URL: <https://www.freebsd.org>
*   Notes:
    *   Runs smoothly on VirtualBox and is beginner-friendly.
    *   Offers a wide range of packages and communities.

### GhostBSD

*   URL: <https://www.ghostbsd.org>
*   Notes:
    *   A user-friendly variant of FreeBSD with a focus on accessibility.
    *   Includes features like accessibility for visually impaired users.

### helloSystem

*   URL: <https://github.com/helloSystem/hello>

### MidnightBSD

*   URL: <https://www.midnightbsd.org>
*   Notes:
    *   A community-developed variant of FreeBSD that aims to provide a more user-friendly experience.
    *   Offers features like improved boot mechanisms and better hardware support.

### NetBSD

*   URL: <https://www.netbsd.org>
*   Notes:
    *   Runs smoothly on VirtualBox and is beginner-friendly.
    *   Includes features like hardware virtualization support.

### NomadBSD

*   URL: <https://nomadbsd.org>
*   Notes:
    *   A variant of BSD that includes a proprietary storage system for laptops.
    *   Designed to improve laptop performance and battery life.

### OpenBSD

*   URL: <https://www.openbsd.org>
*   Notes:
    *   Tends to run slowly on VirtualBox; however, it remains beginner-friendly.
    *   Offers strong security features and is known for its stability.
